behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is parser->hook-handler handoff, not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no real OS/network/subprocess required, no model output, no multi-agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text says only "parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling" (no requirement for real process or OS I/O).
behaviors.test_description: Candidate low (Unit): setup parser function with markdown-heavy JSON string; assert parse success; catches escaping bugs; misses handler-handoff regressions. Candidate current (Integration): setup in-process hook input pipeline (decode -> parse -> input handler) with payload containing code fences, backticks, and $vars; assert parsed object reaches handler unchanged and success path trace/return is normal; catches boundary bugs where parser succeeds but handler rejects/transforms markdown text incorrectly; misses real stdin/process lifecycle issues. Candidate high (System): run hook subprocess via stdin; assert exit/signal; catches process I/O issues; misses nothing extra for this behavior beyond added cost. Pick Integration as lowest that catches stated failure.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: need parser error object + trace formatting/sink contract together. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced in-process; no OS dependency required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text requires trace record content, not real process semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Candidate low (Unit): setup parser-only malformed JSON; assert parse error; catches parser acceptance bugs; misses trace context mapping. Candidate current (Integration): setup malformed payload into in-process parse+trace path; assert `json_parse_failed` emitted with location/context fields identifying malformed section; catches lost offset/snippet mapping between modules; misses subprocess exit/stdio ordering bugs. Candidate high (System): malformed stdin to real process and inspect trace artifact; catches runtime wiring issues; unnecessary for minimum failure boundary here. Pick Integration.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is about mapping across parser error -> policy result -> telemetry intent, a module interaction boundary. REAL-INFRA: local/in-process is sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior explicitly says "local error-policy path" and does not require process/OS behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Candidate low (Unit): test policy mapper function with synthetic parse error; assert handled result; catches pure mapping bugs; misses real parser error-shape mismatch. Candidate current (Integration): run parser with malformed JSON through policy layer; assert no exception escapes, handled result returned, telemetry intent flag set; catches contract drift between parser error and policy branch. Candidate high (System): run full process and inspect exit + telemetry; catches runtime transport issues but exceeds minimum needed for this behavior. Pick Integration.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: explicitly requires "real process execution" and malformed stdin behavior, so subprocess/OS stdio and exit semantics are the failure boundary. MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes can miss crash/ordering/flush issues. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Candidate low (Integration): call entrypoint in-process with malformed buffer; assert handled error; catches logic bugs; misses true process crash and stdio/flush ordering failures. Candidate current (System): spawn hook process, write malformed stdin, capture exit code and trace output/artifact; assert no crash and `json_parse_failed` emitted before exit; catches signal/flush/termination regressions. Candidate high (Agentic): not applicable (no AI output dependency). Pick System.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior text mandates "real hook invocation path" starting at stdin, which is process/OS I/O. MOCK-HIDE: mocked stdin/sink can pass while real invocation wiring drops or reorders trace emission. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Candidate low (Integration): in-process pipeline call with malformed text; assert sink received trace; catches parser-to-sink contract bugs; misses invocation wiring/stdio boundary bugs. Candidate current (System): execute real hook command, feed malformed stdin, assert produced trace artifact includes `json_parse_failed`; catches end-to-end invocation regressions across stdin->parser->sink. Candidate high (Agentic/Workflow): not relevant. Pick System.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires comparing classification/outcome across two paths (valid markdown JSON vs malformed JSON) through shared handling/trace logic. REAL-INFRA: can be done in-process; no mandatory OS/process boundary in behavior text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior asks for attribution distinction, not real subprocess characteristics.
behaviors.test_description: Candidate low (Unit): parser-only parameterized test valid-vs-malformed; assert success/failure; catches core parse regressions; misses trace attribution and policy labeling drift. Candidate current (Integration): run both payload classes through parse+policy+trace modules; assert valid markdown does not emit parse-failure signal and malformed JSON does emit `json_parse_failed`; catches false attribution bugs in cross-module error labeling. Candidate high (System): same via subprocess; catches runtime wiring issues but not required for minimum behavior boundary. Pick Integration.
